summary: An image, with alternative text as a first-class requirement.
use: Always with alt (describing function in context, or alt='' when purely decorative), intrinsic width/height to prevent layout shift, and loading='lazy' below the fold.
avoid: Never lazy-load the hero/LCP image, and never put text in images without mirroring it in alt.
attributes: src / srcset / sizes — sources and responsive selection
fetchpriority — high for the LCP image

summary: Wraps source elements and an img for art direction and format negotiation.
use: To serve AVIF/WebP with fallback, or different crops per viewport; the inner img carries alt and dimensions.
avoid: For plain resolution switching alone, srcset/sizes on img is enough — picture is for format or art changes.

summary: A media resource candidate inside picture, audio, or video.
use: One per format or condition, ordered best-first; the browser takes the first it supports.
avoid: Only valid inside picture, audio, or video; it renders nothing on its own.
snippet:
<video controls width='320' height='180'>
  <source src='/media/intro.webm' type='video/webm'>
  <source src='/media/intro.mp4' type='video/mp4'>
</video>

use: With controls, preload='none' so megabytes don't download speculatively, and a transcript adjacent (details works well).
avoid: Never autoplay with sound; provide fallback content with a download link inside the element.

use: With controls, intrinsic width/height, preload='none' or 'metadata', nested sources per format, and a captions track — non-negotiable for speech.
avoid: Never autoplay with sound; muted looping background video still needs prefers-reduced-motion respect.
snippet:
<video controls width='320' height='180' preload='none'>
  <source src='/media/intro.webm' type='video/webm'>
  <track kind='captions' src='/media/intro.en.vtt' srclang='en' label='English' default>
</video>

summary: Timed text for media: captions, subtitles, descriptions, chapters.
use: kind='captions' with srclang and label on every video containing speech; default preselects one.
avoid: Subtitles translate; captions also transcribe sound effects — pick the kind honestly.
attributes: kind — captions | subtitles | descriptions | chapters | metadata

summary: Defines a client-side image map of clickable regions.
use: Named via the name attribute and referenced from an img's usemap; contains area elements.
avoid: For complex interactive graphics, inline SVG with real links usually beats image maps today.

summary: One clickable region inside an image map.
use: With shape/coords defining the hit area and alt text describing the destination — each area is a link for assistive tech.
avoid: Never omit alt; an unlabeled area is an unlabeled link.
snippet: <area shape='circle' coords='80,50,32' href='#media' alt='The circle — links to the media section'>

summary: A nested browsing context — another document embedded in this one.
use: For embeds you can't inline (maps, videos, sandboxed widgets); always with title for assistive tech and loading='lazy' below the fold.
avoid: Every iframe costs a document fetch and layout — use sparingly, and sandbox untrusted content.
snippet: <iframe title='A minimal embedded document' srcdoc='<p>Hello from inside.</p>' width='320' height='80' loading='lazy'></iframe>

summary: A legacy integration point for external content and plugins.
use: Almost never in new work — historic plugin content only.
avoid: Modern replacements exist for everything it did: iframe, video, audio, img, or object for PDFs.
attributes: src / type — resource and MIME type

summary: An external resource treated as an image, nested context, or plugin resource — with real fallback content.
use: Occasionally for PDFs or SVG-with-fallback, since its children render when the resource can't.
avoid: For images use img/picture; for pages use iframe — object is the niche case.

summary: Inline scalable vector graphics — resolution-independent drawings in the DOM.
use: Inline for icons and diagrams; give meaningful graphics role='img' and an internal title element for the accessible name.
avoid: Purely decorative inline SVG duplicating adjacent text gets aria-hidden='true' instead.
snippet:
<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='120' height='40' viewBox='0 0 120 40' role='img' aria-label='Three ascending bars'>
  <rect x='10' y='22' width='24' height='14'/>
  <rect x='48' y='12' width='24' height='24'/>
  <rect x='86' y='4' width='24' height='32'/>
</svg>

summary: MathML Core — native mathematical notation in the browser.
use: For formulas that should be text: selectable, searchable, styleable, read correctly by screen readers.
avoid: Don't screenshot equations into images — they can't reflow, scale, or be read aloud.

summary: A scriptable bitmap surface for dynamic graphics, games, and visualizations.
use: Only with JavaScript driving it; provide fallback content inside, and prefer SVG when the graphic is document-like.
avoid: Canvas content is invisible to assistive technology and search — never render text or UI into it that has no DOM counterpart.
snippet: Fallback: your browser can't render the chart; the data is in the table below.

summary: An iframe variant with the communication channel cut — the embedder can load content but never learn what was shown.
use: Privacy Sandbox ad slots chosen by Protected Audience auctions or Shared Storage; the config is set via JavaScript, never a URL.
avoid: Chromium-only and experimental; for any general embedding use iframe.
